reasoning: The task of building a full-stack todo app naturally decomposes into four distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. Schema design is a prerequisite for both backend and frontend. Backend and frontend can be developed in parallel once the schema is defined. Integration tests depend on both backend and frontend being complete. I will spawn four subtasks to handle these phases, allocating budget appropriately.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the data model for todos, including fields like id, title, completed status, and timestamps. It serves as the foundation for both backend API and frontend state management.

outputs.description: JSON or SQL definition of the todo database schema.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the server-side logic, including CRUD endpoints for todos, based on the schema. It enables the frontend to interact with persistent data.

outputs.description: Source code for the backend API server.
Documentation of API endpoints and request/response formats.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates the user interface for managing todos, including forms, lists, and status toggles. It consumes the backend API to provide a complete user experience.

outputs.description: Source code for the frontend application.

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the full-stack todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask verifies that the frontend and backend work together correctly by testing end-to-end user flows. It ensures the entire system functions as intended.

outputs.description: Test scripts that validate the full-stack application.
